question: The compact three-dimensional U(1) lattice gauge theory (3D U(1) LGT) is studied on an asymmetric lattice L^2 x N_t at vanishing spatial coupling (beta_s=0). Spatial link variables are integrated out exactly, reducing the model to a two-dimensional effective spin model for Polyakov-loop angles. For N_t=1, the reduced model is the exact 2D XY model. Monte Carlo simulations on spatial lattices up to L=256 with finite-size scaling are used. The Svetitsky-Yaffe conjecture predicts 2D XY universality for this gauge theory. What is the ORDER of the finite-temperature deconfinement phase transition of the compact 3D U(1) lattice gauge theory at beta_s=0? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt". Also include "universality_class" using one of: "2D XY", "BKT", "2D Ising", "3D XY", "other".

metadata.system: Compact 3D U(1) lattice gauge theory on L^2 x N_t lattice at beta_s=0, deconfinement transition
metadata.source_excerpt: The compact three-dimensional U(1) lattice gauge theory at finite temperature on a Lambda=L^2 x N_t lattice with vanishing spatial coupling beta_s=0... belongs to the 2D XY universality class (BKT transition), confirming the Svetitsky-Yaffe conjecture
metadata.difficulty_note: BKT confirmed by essential-singularity scaling of pseudo-critical coupling and eta=1/4 at criticality; N_t=8 has remaining ambiguity between BKT and first-order scaling.
